message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step approach:
- Read the main GDPR nodes and their 1-hop cross-references supplied.
- Read the policy chunk (marketing by post and email; reliance on Art 6(1)(f); right to object; retention conditions).
- For each main node, check the entire chunk for any explicit statements that alter, negate, narrow, expand, or reorder what the GDPR requires. Per instructions, omissions are treated as Non-Deviation.
- Identify and list cross-references per node from the referenced_nodes set and consider them in the assessment.

Per-node reasoning:
- REG-001 (Art6(1)): Policy explicitly bases marketing on legitimate interests under Art 6(1)(f). No contradictory or narrowing statements about lawfulness. No deviation.
- REG-004 (Art6(4) + refs Art23(1), Art9/10): Policy describes further use for direct marketing but does not assert incompatibility or waive the compatibility test; it simply states reliance on Art 6(1)(f). As omissions are non-deviations, and no contradiction is present, no deviation.
- REG-012 (Art13(1) + refs Art6(1)(f), Arts46,47,49(1)): The chunk supplies purpose and legal basis and an objection note. It does not contradict Art13(1) requirements; missing items (e.g., recipients, DPO, transfers) are omissions, not deviations. No deviation.
- REG-018 (Art14(3) + refs Art14(1)-(2)): The described data seem collected from the data subject in connection with a sale; Art14 duties are not directly implicated and no conflicting statement appears. Omissions are non-deviations. No deviation.
- REG-019 (Art14(4) + ref Art14(2)): No assertion about further processing communications contrary to Art14(4). Omissions are non-deviations. No deviation.
- REG-023 (Art15(3)): The policy chunk does not address access copies at all; no contradictory claim. No deviation.
- REG-035 (Art21(1) + ref Art6(1)(e)/(f)): The chunk affirms the right to object at any time for the marketing use based on Art 6(1)(f). It does not state that processing would continue despite objection (which for direct marketing would be prohibited under Art21(3) anyway). No deviation.
- REG-036 (Art21(2)): The chunk allows objection “at any time” to direct marketing (at least for email and arguably postal as well). No deviation.
- REG-037 (Art21(3)): The chunk indicates marketing will not continue where there is an effective objection (“unless you have objected”; retention only until objection). No deviation.
- REG-087 (Art49(2) + ref Art49(1)(g)): The chunk does not discuss public registers or third-country transfers; no conflicting statement. No deviation.
</deliberation>
